refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide a robust and auditable method to automatically derive, record, and manage performance requirements (e.g., cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from worksite mission context, ensuring traceability to worksite inputs and maintaining a history of baselines/calibration updates.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is clear but can be strengthened by adding 'robust and auditable' to reflect the high safety criticality and the need for documented performance targets. 'Automatically' is added to imply system-driven derivation, reducing manual error. Explicitly mentioning 'traceability to worksite inputs' and 'history of baselines/calibration updates' directly addresses the system constraints.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by demonstrating the system's ability to ingest various worksite mission contexts, automatically generate a set of performance requirements, record them in a persistent store, and allow for retrieval and auditing of historical baselines and calibration updates. Test cases should cover different mission types and parameter changes.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1 (Performance Requirement Derivation): The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements based on worksite mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.2 (Performance Requirement Recording): The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements and their historical baselines/calibration updates.
CR-NGHE-005.3 (Performance Requirement Management): The NGHE solution shall provide capabilities to manage (e.g., retrieve, audit, update) performance requirements and their history.

candidate_dps: A rule-based inference engine that processes worksite mission parameters to generate performance targets, stored in a version-controlled database.
A machine learning model trained on historical worksite data to predict optimal performance targets, with results stored in a data lake.
Manual input and approval workflow for performance targets, stored in a document management system.
selected_dp.selected_dp: A rule-based inference engine that processes worksite mission parameters to generate performance targets, stored in a version-controlled database.

coupling_check.notes: The rule-based engine provides clear, auditable logic, which is crucial for high safety criticality. The version-controlled database ensures traceability and history management. This DP minimizes coupling by centralizing the derivation logic and storage.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite mission context can be sufficiently formalized into rules for the inference engine.
Assumption: A suitable version-controlled database technology is available and integrates well with other system components.
Risk: Complexity of rule definition and maintenance could become high for diverse mission contexts.
Risk: Performance of the inference engine for real-time derivation might be a concern for very complex rule sets.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The overarching system responsible for integrating all functionalities.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Input of worksite mission context parameters (e.g., machine status, environmental conditions) for performance derivation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Output of derived performance targets to other system components for operational guidance or control.

module_containment_scores.notes: This FR/DP is core to the overall NGHE Solution's value proposition. It defines a fundamental capability that orchestrates other modules. High containment, low diffusion as the core logic resides here.
The Vehicle module might consume performance targets but is not responsible for their derivation or management. Low containment for derivation, but high diffusion for consumption.
Teleoperation might display performance targets or use them for remote control, but it doesn't derive or manage them. Low containment for derivation, moderate diffusion for display/use.

Safety Services might use performance targets (e.g., for safety limit checking) and might even influence their derivation (e.g., safety-critical performance limits). Some containment for influencing, high diffusion for consumption.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The 'NGHE Solution' module is the most appropriate owner for the core logic of deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements, as this is a foundational capability that orchestrates and informs other modules. It acts as the central intelligence for performance optimization.
containment_risks: Risk: If the 'NGHE Solution' becomes a monolithic module, it could lead to high internal coupling and reduced maintainability.
Risk: Clear internal interfaces within 'NGHE Solution' must be defined to prevent 'god object' anti-pattern.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (rule-based inference engine with version-controlled database) directly addresses all aspects of the refined FR, including derivation, recording, management, traceability, and history. It also aligns with the 'robust and auditable' aspect for high safety criticality.

diffusion_risks: The derived performance targets will need to be diffused to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. This requires robust and well-defined interfaces (e.g., Tasking, Telemetry) to ensure consistent interpretation and use across the system.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The 'NGHE Solution' module is a logical initial home for this core capability. However, as the system evolves, sub-modules within 'NGHE Solution' might be needed to further decompose the derivation, recording, and management functions to prevent bloat.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the refined FR against the 'NGHE Solution' module is feasible. The module can be tested to ensure it correctly derives, records, and manages performance requirements based on various inputs and that the outputs are traceable and auditable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation is clear: the 'NGHE Solution' module is responsible for the entire lifecycle of performance requirement derivation and management. Other modules are consumers or providers of data to this core function.

decision.justification: The FR has been refined and decomposed into three sub-FRs, and a suitable DP and IM have been identified for the top-level FR. The gates have passed, indicating a consistent mapping. The next logical step is to decompose the sub-FRs identified in the refinement step to further detail the system's functionality.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1 (Performance Requirement Derivation): The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements based on worksite mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.2 (Performance Requirement Recording): The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements and their historical baselines/calibration updates.
CR-NGHE-005.3 (Performance Requirement Management): The NGHE solution shall provide capabilities to manage (e.g., retrieve, audit, update) performance requirements and their history.

assumptions: Worksite mission context can be sufficiently formalized into rules for the inference engine.
A suitable version-controlled database technology is available and integrates well with other system components.
open_risks: Complexity of rule definition and maintenance could become high for diverse mission contexts.
Performance of the inference engine for real-time derivation might be a concern for very complex rule sets.
If the 'NGHE Solution' becomes a monolithic module, it could lead to high internal coupling and reduced maintainability.
Clear internal interfaces within 'NGHE Solution' must be defined to prevent 'god object' anti-pattern.
evidence_needed: Formalized examples of worksite mission contexts and corresponding performance requirements to validate rule feasibility.
Proof-of-concept for the rule-based inference engine and version-controlled database integration.
Architectural design for internal structure of 'NGHE Solution' to mitigate monolithic risks.
